project_privacy_data_flows.data_type: Location Data (CLLocation: latitude, longitude for nearby article search), User Location Authorization Status (CLAuthorizationStatus)
project_privacy_data_flows.description: User's device location is used to search for Wikipedia articles about nearby places. Location data is sent to the MediaWiki API for geospatial article search.

flow_steps.code: public func performSearch(_ search: PlaceSearch, defaultSiteURL: URL, region: MKCoordinateRegion, completion: @escaping (PlaceSearchResult) -> Void) { locationSearchFetcher.search(...) }
flow_steps.details: User opens the Places tab or performs a nearby article search. The app searches for Wikipedia articles near the user's current location.

flow_steps.code: public func startMonitoringLocation() { guard authorizationStatus.isAuthorized else { ... return }; locationManager.startUpdatingLocation(); startUpdatingHeading(); isUpdating = true }
flow_steps.details: LocationManager requests the device's current location from CoreLocation. The app must have already received location authorization from the user.

flow_steps.code: Coordinates are sent to the MediaWiki API 'query' with 'list=geosearch' to find nearby articles
flow_steps.details: The user's latitude and longitude are sent to the Wikipedia MediaWiki API as query parameters (list=geosearch) to find articles about places near the user's location.

flow_steps.code: UICollectionView displays nearby article results on a map and list
flow_steps.details: Nearby article results are displayed on a map and in a list, showing the distance from the user's location to each article.

project_privacy_data_flows.data_type: Location Authorization Status (CLAuthorizationStatus: notDetermined, restricted, denied, authorizedAlways, authorizedWhenInUse)
project_privacy_data_flows.description: The app requests location permission when the user first accesses the Places tab for nearby article discovery.

flow_steps.details: When location monitoring is requested but authorization status is .notDetermined, LocationManager triggers the system location permission dialog.

flow_steps.code: iOS shows the system location permission dialog to the user
flow_steps.details: The user is prompted to grant 'When In Use' location permission. The result is stored by iOS.

flow_steps.code: Cell displaying location authorization request UI when permission hasn't been granted
flow_steps.details: If location permission is denied, a banner cell is shown in the Places tab inviting the user to enable location access in Settings.
